summary: Cross-entity search across briefs, customers, and notes. Read-heavy, sync, single-store.

Architecture.title: Service pattern
Architecture.rationale: Read-heavy, low write volume; sync keeps the API surface narrow.

Data Model.title: Storage engine
Data Model.decision: Postgres with JSONB for flexible search columns.
Data Model.rationale: Single store; tsvector + JSONB cover today's search needs.

tiers.enterprise: TLS to PG, encrypted at rest

API Design.title: Query endpoint
API Design.rationale: REST keeps clients simple; GET is cacheable.

Security.title: Authn
Security.rationale: Short-lived JWTs match the tier mandate (NIST 800-63B); vault provides rotation.

Performance.title: Caching
Performance.rationale: Search hits are repetitive; 60s gives stale-data tolerance for the use case.

tiers.federal: Redis must be local; no cloud egress

open_questions: What's the canonical typo-tolerance approach — pg_trgm or postgres ILIKE on prefix?
Do customer notes need separate ACL filtering or is brief-level enough?
